This is a cycle detection problem in a directed graph. If there's a cycle, you cannot finish all courses.
Build an adjacency list from the prerequisites. Then do a DFS from every node.
Track visit state with three states: 0 = unvisited, 1 = visiting (in current DFS path), 2 = visited (done). If you visit a node with state 1, there's a cycle.
Alternatively, use Kahn's algorithm (BFS topological sort with in-degree counting). If all nodes are processed, no cycle exists.
